message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Yard House (Rancho Mirage) (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: 1,200+ on Yelp (1,207 reviews); 670+ on Tripadvisor; Google rating reported ~4.4 (count [UNVERIFIED] from direct Google fetch)
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Operating at The River since the mid-2000s as part of the chain's CA footprint; chain founded 1996 in Long Beach, acquired by Darden Restaurants in 2012 for $585M. Specific Rancho Mirage opening year [UNVERIFIED] — The River shopping center itself opened 2002, so the landlord-page claim of a 1996 Rancho Mirage opening conflates the chain's founding with this location.
- Credentials: []
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably in 'restaurants at The River Rancho Mirage' and tenant-directory queries (Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Yelp, The River's own site). Does NOT surface in 'best craft beer Coachella Valley' / 'best breweries Palm Springs' queries despite the chain's 100+ tap positioning — those are dominated by Coachella Valley Brewing, La Quinta Brewing, Las Palmas Brewing. Absent from 'date night Rancho Mirage' editorial lists (Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, Shame On The Moon, Foxy's dominate). Absent from Palm Springs Life '10 Great Places to Eat in Rancho Mirage'-style editorial roundups.
- Site structured data: Chain-operated location subpage at yardhouse.com/locations/ca/rancho-mirage/rancho-mirage-the-river/8304 (Darden infrastructure) — clean address/phone/hours and menu links typical of Darden-tier local SEO. Direct WebFetch of the location URL returned empty in this session [UNVERIFIED schema.org markup presence], but the location is well-syndicated to Yelp, Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Foursquare, Untappd, BeerAdvocate, Sirved, Gayot, Wanderlog, Restaurantji — agent-readable footprint is broad.
- Visibility gap score: 3
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Yard House Rancho Mirage at The River:

## Restaurant Overview

Yard House is an upscale, casual eatery known for great food, classic rock music, an energetic vibe and endless fleet of tap handles featuring the best American craft and import beers. This Yard House is located at The River, which is basically something like a Universal City Walk, only much smaller.

## Yelp Reviews Summary

The Yelp page shows 1,207 reviews with 1,064 photos for the location at 71800 Ca-111 Space B-101, Rancho Mirage, CA, with operating hours from 11:00 am to 12:00 am Monday through Thursday and Sunday, and 11:00 am to 1:00 am Friday and Saturday.

## Positive Reviews

The Palm Springs (Rancho Mirage) location truly feels like a neighborhood pub with good food, great salads, awesome onion rings, and a Nashville Chicken sandwich described as sweet, spicy, and perfectly cooked. Service is typically fast and organized, even during busy times, with staff attentive to orders, and the restaurant is known for its great food and friendly service.

## Menu & Highlights

The globally inspired menu features more than 100 made-from-scratch favorites, from signature starters like Poke Nachos and Fried Mac + Cheese, to street tacos, grilled burgers, pizza, seafood and steak.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best craft beer breweries in the Coachella Valley:

## Top Breweries

**Coachella Valley Brewing Company, which has been operating in Thousand Palms since 2013, has 14 taps of its own beer with plenty of rotation**. They are constantly trying out new recipes and strive to use local ingredients from all over the Coachella Valley.

**Desert Beer Company in Palm Desert opened in 2019, with 15 taps across a wide variety of styles**. The brewery is owned & operated by Devon Sanchez, who previously worked at Coachella Valley Brewing Company.

**La Quinta Brewing Company has been open since 2013 and maintains three taprooms in the Coachella Valley — in Palm Desert, Palm Springs, and La Quinta**.

**Located at Spotlight 29 Casino in Coachella, this five-barrel brewhouse keeps a rotating selection of 29 beers on tap (including third-party labels)**.

**Luchador Brewing Company in Cathedral City features a vibrant selection of craft beers with hoppy IPAs, brown ales and refreshing lagers**.

The Coachella Valley is having a brewery renaissance, with several excellent options for craft beer enthusiasts throughout the region.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best date night restaurants in Rancho Mirage:

## Fine Dining Options

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - A Rancho Mirage institution that delivers timeless elegance with mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents. It features a stellar menu crafted by French chef Jean-Louis Jalouneix and candlelit ambiance with twinkling chandeliers.

**The Edge Steakhouse** - Located at The Ritz-Carlton, this refined signature restaurant has floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the sprawling desert landscape.

**Willie's Modern Fare** - This chic restaurant blends classic sophistication with contemporary cuisine, offering seared scallops with citrus beurre blanc and handcrafted cocktails, making it an ideal go-to for date nights.

## International Cuisine

**Shabu Shabu Zen** - This family-owned gem specializes in Japanese hot pot with an interactive culinary experience featuring premium Wagyu beef and fresh seafood, making it ideal for intimate date nights.

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar** - A family-run spot serving steaks and osso buco, complemented by seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

## Casual-Upscale Options

**Si Bon Belgian Bistro** - A minimalist bistro with an excellent French-Belgian menu available at breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

**Las Casuelas Nuevas** - A family-owned Mexican restaurant featuring a lush hacienda-style setting with colorful tilework, twinkling lights, and a buzzing patio perfect for margaritas and live music.

# Yard House Rancho Mirage Opening Year

The page does not specify when Yard House opened at The River location in Rancho Mirage. 

The only historical information provided is about the Yard House chain itself: "Founded in Long Beach, Calif., in 1996, Yard House now has 71 restaurants across 24 states." This refers to the company's founding, not the opening of this specific Rancho Mirage location.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Yard House in the Palm Desert area:

## About Yard House

Yard House is the modern American gathering place where beer and food lovers unite, known for great food, classic rock, an energetic vibe and endless fleet of tap handles featuring the best craft and local beers, with each location offering 100+ beers on tap along with an array of craft cocktails.

## Location and Details

The Yard House in the Palm Desert area is located at 71800 Ca-111 Space B-101 in Rancho Mirage, and is open Monday through Thursday from 11:00 am - 12:00 am, Friday and Saturday from 11:00 am - 1:00 am, and Sunday from 11:00 am - 12:00 am.

## Menu

The menu features more than 100 made-from-scratch favorites, including signature starters like Poke Nachos and Fried Mac + Cheese, street tacos, grilled burgers, pizza, seafood and steak.

## Customer Experience

Reviews highlight the unbelievable selection of draft beers, great food and awesome bartenders, with signature dishes like the Mango Cart, Ahi Stack, and Blackened Chicken Caesar salad being popular choices. The restaurant offers a great happy hour on weekdays and late-night happy hour Sunday through Thursday.

content.text: I have enough independent verification. Let me finalize.

confirms: Yelp review count: 1,207 reviews confirmed on Yelp page title (search-result snippet matches the 1,200+ claim)
Tripadvisor review volume: independent search returned 678 reviews, matching the 670+ claim
Sentiment is mostly positive — Yelp and Tripadvisor snippets emphasize fast service, great food, energetic vibe, popular happy hour
Chain founded 1996 in Long Beach — confirmed on The River's own tenant page ('Founded in Long Beach, Calif., in 1996, Yard House now has 71 restaurants across 24 states')

Does NOT surface in 'best craft beer Coachella Valley / breweries' queries — those results are dominated by Coachella Valley Brewing, La Quinta Brewing, Desert Beer Company, Luchador, Spotlight 29 — Yard House is absent despite its 100+ tap positioning

Does NOT surface in 'date night Rancho Mirage' editorial roundups — Locale Magazine, Palm Springs Life, Visit Greater Palm Springs lists feature Wally's Desert Turtle, The Edge Steakhouse, Willie's Modern Fare, Shabu Shabu Zen, Foxy's, Si Bon, Las Casuelas Nuevas — Yard House absent

Surfaces reliably in tenant-directory / location queries (Yelp, Tripadvisor, BeerAdvocate, Foursquare, Untappd, Restaurantji, Gayot, The River's own site, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Rancho Mirage Chamber)
Landlord-page conflation of the 1996 chain founding with the Rancho Mirage location is plausible — The River's own Yard House page only cites the 1996 chain founding, not a local opening year
flagged_as_unverified: Google rating ~4.4 and Google review count — not independently fetched in this session

Specific Rancho Mirage opening year — The River's tenant page only cites the 1996 chain founding; a BeerAdvocate review from Jan 2007 calls it 'a terrific addition' suggesting roughly 2006-2007, but no authoritative source confirms an exact year (consistent with the brief's own [UNVERIFIED] flag)

JSON-LD / schema.org structured data on yardhouse.com/locations/ca/rancho-mirage/... — WebFetch returned empty content (likely client-side rendered or bot-blocked), cannot confirm or deny presence of Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup
Darden 2012 acquisition price of $585M — widely reported historically but not re-verified this session
Chain footprint number (71 locations per landlord page vs. broader chain claims) — landlord-page figure may be stale
input.notes: The core visibility thesis holds cleanly under independent search. Yelp 1,207 and Tripadvisor 678 are both confirmed verbatim in search results. The two key negative-space claims — absence from 'best craft beer Coachella Valley' and absence from 'date night Rancho Mirage' editorial — are both independently reproduced: brewery queries surface CVB / La Quinta / Desert Beer / Luchador, and date-night queries surface Wally's / Edge Steakhouse / Willie's / Foxy's / Si Bon — Yard House appears in none of the editorial top-10 lists I sampled. Visibility gap score of 3 and anchor-reference tier are consistent with this pattern: the location is heavily syndicated to aggregators and the landlord directory, but absent from human-curated editorial lists. The schema.org claim is the only meaningful gap — WebFetch couldn't read the location page (returned empty), so neither presence nor absence of JSON-LD can be confirmed here; the brief already flags this as [UNVERIFIED] which is the right posture. Opening-year [UNVERIFIED] flag is also appropriately handled — the landlord page itself only cites the 1996 chain founding, supporting the brief's conflation observation.
